exam_price_notes: $93 exam fee + ~$30 test center fee. Fee waivers for military/low-income.
target_audience: Students who have completed a one-year college general biology course
recommended_experience: Completion of a one-year college general biology course

prerequisites.description: Completion of a one-year college general biology course

retake_policy.notes: Each institution reserves the right to set its own credit-granting policy, which may differ from the American Council on Education (ACE). Contact your college to find out the score required for credit and the number of credit hours granted.

objectives.title: Chemical structure of carbohydrates, lipids, proteins, and nucleic acids
Cells
sub_objectives: Structure and function of cell organelles
Comparison of prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells

objectives.title: Energy transformations
sub_objectives: Glycolysis, cellular respiration, aerobic and anaerobic pathways

objectives.title: Cell division
sub_objectives: Mitosis, meiosis, and cytokinesis in plants and animals

objectives.title: Chemical nature of the gene
sub_objectives: Control of protein synthesis: transcription, translation, and post-transcriptional processing

objectives.title: Structure and function in plants with emphasis on angiosperms
sub_objectives: Root, stem, leaf, flower, seed, and fruit
Water and mineral absorption and transport
Alternation of generations in ferns, conifers, and flowering plants

objectives.title: Structure and function in animals with emphasis on vertebrates
sub_objectives: Major systems (e.g., digestive, gas exchange, skeletal, nervous, circulatory, excretory, and immune)
Hormonal control in homeostasis and reproduction
Cleavage, gastrulation, germ layer formation, and differentiation of organ systems
Formation and function of the mammalian placenta
Blood circulation in the human embryo

objectives.title: Principles of ecology
sub_objectives: Energy flow and productivity in ecosystems
Population growth and regulation (natality, mortality, competition, migration, density, r- and K-selection)
Community structure, growth, and regulation (major biomes and succession)

objectives.title: Principles of evolution
sub_objectives: Concepts of natural selection (differential reproduction, mutation, Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium, speciation, and punctuated equilibrium)
Major features of plant and animal evolution
Convergence, extinction, balanced polymorphism, and genetic drift

objectives.title: Human population growth
sub_objectives: Age composition, birth and fertility rates, and theory of demographic transition

objectives.title: Human intervention in the natural world
sub_objectives: Management of resources, and environmental pollution
Biomedical progress (control of human reproduction, and genetic engineering)

official_study_resources.title: CLEP Biology Examination Guide
official_study_resources.notes: The Biology exam covers molecular and cellular biology, organismal biology, and population biology.

official_study_resources.title: Study Resources: Biology
official_study_resources.notes: A study plan and list of online resources.

official_study_resources.title: Sample Questions: Biology
official_study_resources.notes: Answer sample questions related to the Biology exam.

official_study_resources.title: What Your CLEP Score Means
official_study_resources.notes: Guide to understanding how CLEP scores are calculated and credit-granting recommendations for all exams.

official_study_resources.title: ACE Credit Recommendations
official_study_resources.notes: Recommendations for credit-granting scores from the American Council on Education.
